name: hig-components-search
description: Apple HIG guidance for navigation-related components including search fields, page controls, and path controls. Helps developers implement search UX, pagination, and breadcrumb navigation following Apple's design standards.
capabilities: Provides Apple Human Interface Guidelines for search fields including scope controls, tokens, and instant results
Explains page control patterns for flat pagination with dot indicators and platform-specific behaviors
Documents path controls for macOS file hierarchy navigation and breadcrumbs
Covers platform differences for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS
Includes best practices for search placement, focus behavior, and result filtering
References official Apple API documentation for SwiftUI and UIKit implementations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 200 static analysis findings are false positives from pattern matching on Markdown documentation content. This skill contains only Apple Human Interface Guidelines reference documentation with no executable code. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are inline code formatting (backticks in Markdown), 'network' patterns are legitimate documentation links to developer.apple.com, and 'blocker' patterns match words in UI component names and API references. This is safe documentation content.
valueStatement: Get Apple-approved search field, page control, and path control implementations that follow Human Interface Guidelines. Ensure your app's navigation feels native and intuitive across iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms.
limitations: Contains design guidance only - does not generate code implementations
Limited to Apple platforms - does not cover Android or web navigation patterns
Content is static reference documentation - not interactive or customizable
Requires developer to interpret guidelines and implement components themselves

useCases.title: Implement Search in iOS App
useCases.description: An iOS developer needs to add search functionality to their app. They use this skill to understand whether to place search in a tab bar, toolbar, or inline, and how to implement scope controls for filtering results.

useCases.title: Add Pagination to Photo Gallery
useCases.description: A developer building a photo gallery app needs page controls for navigation. They consult this skill to learn proper page control placement, indicator customization, and platform-specific behaviors for iOS vs watchOS.

useCases.targetUser: macOS developer creating file management UI
useCases.title: Design macOS File Browser
useCases.description: A Mac developer is building a file browser and needs path controls for breadcrumb navigation. They reference this skill to understand standard vs popup path controls and proper placement in window body vs toolbar.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Search Guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: Where should I place the search field in my iOS app? The app has a tab bar at the bottom.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer starting search implementation needs placement advice

promptTemplates.title: Platform-Specific Page Controls
promptTemplates.prompt: How do page controls differ between iOS and watchOS? I'm building a fitness app that targets both platforms.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer needs to understand platform variations for pagination

promptTemplates.title: Scope Controls and Tokens
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm building a Mail-like app. How do I implement scope controls and tokens for search filtering?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer implementing advanced search filtering features

promptTemplates.title: Path Control Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to show file hierarchy navigation in a macOS document app. Should I use standard or popup path controls, and where should I place them?
promptTemplates.scenario: Mac developer needs breadcrumb navigation design guidance

bestPractices: Start search immediately when users type to provide instant, continuously refined results rather than waiting for form submission
Place search fields where users expect them - typically top of list, toolbar, or navigation bar - with helpful placeholder text describing what can be searched
Default to broader search scope and let users narrow it with scope controls, providing context for the full result set before refinement
antiPatterns: Avoid using generic placeholder text like 'Search' in search fields - it provides no helpful context about what content can be searched
Do not use page controls for hierarchical navigation - they are designed only for flat, linear page sequences where all pages are equally weighted
Never place path controls in toolbars or status bars - they belong in the window body, like Finder's path bar at the bottom of the window

faq.question: What platforms does this skill cover?
faq.answer: This skill covers all Apple platforms: iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS. It documents platform-specific considerations and behaviors for search fields, page controls, and path controls where applicable.

faq.question: Does this skill provide code examples?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides design guidance and best practices from Apple's Human Interface Guidelines. It references official Apple API documentation for SwiftUI and UIKit implementations but does not generate code itself.

faq.question: What's the difference between scope controls and tokens?
faq.answer: Scope controls are segmented controls that let users choose a search category before or during search (like All vs Current mailbox in Mail). Tokens are visual representations of specific search terms that act as filters (like filtering by a specific contact). You can use both together for powerful search filtering.

faq.question: When should I use focused vs unfocused search in a tab bar?
faq.answer: Use focused search (keyboard appears immediately) when you want quick, transient search experiences. Use unfocused search (field appears but keyboard waits) to promote discovery and exploration, especially in apps with large content collections like Music or TV.

faq.question: Can I use path controls on iOS?
faq.answer: No, path controls are macOS-only according to Apple's HIG. For iOS file hierarchy navigation, consider using navigation controllers, tab bars, or sidebars instead to represent hierarchical relationships.

faq.question: How many page indicators are too many?
faq.answer: Apple recommends avoiding more than about 10 page indicators because they become hard to count at a glance. If your app needs more than 10 pages as peers, consider using a different arrangement like a grid that lets people navigate content in any order.
